checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the text found in the source document. No fabricated sentences or paragraphs were identified. The captions and summaries added to the tables are allowed under the 'Acceptable Changes' criteria for accessibility.
The source document does not contain any contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, or URLs), and none were added to the output.
All sections and pages from the source PDF (2 pages, 10 numbered questions, title, and footer) are present in the output JSON.

The source document contains two main table structures (one on each page). The first table has 5 rows (questions 1-5) and the second has 5 rows (questions 6-10). The output correctly represents these as two tables with 5 rows each.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text content within each cell and section is complete. No substantial content was omitted. Minor line-break artifacts (e.g., 'sum marize' becoming 'summarize') were correctly handled.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output faithfully preserves the wording and formatting (bold/italics) of the source. For example, it correctly preserved the typo 'will you will' in question 5 which appears in the source image.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly captures each of the 10 questions and the footer once. There is no evidence of duplicated sections, paragraphs, or table rows. The title is appropriately used as both the metadata title and the primary section heading, which is expected behavior.

The document structure is flat, consistent with the source. The title 'GRANT PROJECT PLANNING WORKSHEET' is correctly assigned as the top-level section title. There are no subheadings in the source, and the output does not incorrectly fabricate any.

The source document's grid layout (Question Number | Question Text | Answer Space) has been faithfully converted into two table elements (one for each page). Each row contains three cells corresponding to these three columns. Empty answer spaces are correctly represented as empty strings in the third cell of each row.
The reading order follows the logical flow of the original document: Title -> Questions 1-5 (Table 1) -> Questions 6-10 (Table 2) -> Footer paragraph.
The source document contains no formal lists; the numbered items are part of a table-like grid structure which has been correctly modeled as a table.
The source document contains no hyperlinks, and no links have been injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF utilizes a vertical layout with color-coded sidebars for numbering and large open boxes for text input across two pages. The HTML output adapts this into a single-page view containing two clearly labeled tables (Questions 1-5 and 6-10). Although the visual styling is simplified, the organization of information remains faithful to the source's intent; the questions are presented in the same order and are logically grouped. This is a standard and acceptable conversion for accessibility.

The HTML output presents the worksheet questions within tables that are correctly rendered. The borders are intact, columns are aligned, and the text within the cells is clearly readable. The inclusion of an empty third column effectively mirrors the blank response areas from the original PDF worksheet. There is no overlapping or visual corruption.

The source PDF does not contain any meaningful visual information such as logos, photos, or diagrams. The number blocks are decorative. Consequently, there are no missing or broken meaningful images in the HTML conversion.
Upon visual inspection of the HTML screenshot, no body content (such as the questions or the instructional text) is duplicated. Each numbered question appears exactly once in its respective table.
